title: Out-File without -Encoding defaults to UCS-2 LE (UTF-16) in PS 5.1
description: In PowerShell 5.1, Out-File defaults to Unicode (UCS-2 LE / UTF-16) encoding when -Encoding is not specified. This doubles file size compared to UTF-8, creates compatibility issues with tools expecting UTF-8, and produces files that are inconsistent with Set-Content (which defaults to ANSI). The -Encoding UTF8 parameter should always be specified explicitly to ensure consistent, portable output.
detection_method: Regex scan for Out-File calls missing -Encoding parameter
preventionRule: Always specify -Encoding UTF8 on Out-File. PS 5.1 default is UCS-2 LE (UTF-16), not UTF-8.
remedy: Add -Encoding UTF8 parameter to all Out-File calls. Example: Get-Process | Out-File -FilePath $path -Encoding UTF8
